ground_truth.title: Certificate in Accounting - Hunter College Continuing Education
ground_truth.main_content:
Certificates in New York City for Accounting Professionals

Certificate in Accounting

The Accounting Certificate Program develops students’ abilities to understand business transactions and financial statements and to determine the most appropriate financial measures for those events. Choose from a range of courses tailored to meet your specific professional goals. Earn professional credentials and accelerate your career with the critical skills you need to succeed in the industry. This program is approved by the National Association of Certified Public Bookkeepers (NACPB).

Program Information

Join us for an Open House for the Accounting Certificate on Wednesday, February 11, at 6:00 pm. Click here to sign up.
Duration of the program: The program consists of 4 courses and can be completed in 2 or 3 semesters, depending on scheduling.
Tuition: $2,650 plus a one-time $25 registration fee
Payment Plan: Down payment of $1,455 + 2 payments of $610
Brochure: Schedule and Books

Program Information and Schedule

Courses and Program Details

Bookkeeping and Accounting I
This course introduces the fundamental principles of accounting and bookkeeping. Students will learn how to record financial transactions, prepare financial statements, and understand the accounting cycle. Topics include the accounting equation, double-entry bookkeeping, journal entries, ledgers, trial balances, and the preparation of basic financial statements (income statement, balance sheet, and statement of cash flows). Emphasis is placed on practical application through hands-on exercises and real-world examples.

Bookkeeping and Accounting II
Building on the foundations of Bookkeeping and Accounting I, this course delves deeper into advanced bookkeeping techniques and accounting principles. Students will explore topics such as adjusting entries, closing entries, payroll accounting, inventory valuation, and depreciation methods. The course also covers the preparation of more complex financial statements and introduces basic financial analysis. Practical exercises and case studies are used to reinforce concepts and develop proficiency in accounting software applications.

QuickBooks Online
This hands-on course provides comprehensive training in QuickBooks Online, a leading cloud-based accounting software. Students will learn how to set up a company file, manage chart of accounts, record transactions, generate invoices, process payments, reconcile bank accounts, and produce financial reports. The course covers both basic and advanced features of QuickBooks Online, including payroll processing, inventory management, and budgeting. By the end of the course, students will be proficient in using QuickBooks Online to manage the financial operations of a small business.

Payroll Accounting
This course focuses on the principles and practices of payroll accounting. Students will learn how to calculate gross pay, deductions, and net pay for employees. Topics include federal and state payroll tax regulations, Social Security and Medicare taxes, unemployment taxes, and wage and hour laws. The course also covers payroll recordkeeping, reporting requirements, and the use of payroll software. Emphasis is placed on compliance with legal requirements and accurate payroll processing.
